channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Zomato's Growth Surge: High Valuation Meets Tough Competition
item.description: India's leading food delivery platforms, including Zomato, experienced a significant growth rebound in the October-December quarter. This surge was attributed to festive demand, value-focused customer propositions, and expanding user bases, signaling sustained momentum into the upcoming quarters. Zomato's food delivery segment saw net order value grow by 16.6% year-on-year, contributing to a 21.3% increase in gross order value, with monthly transacting users rising 21% to 24.9 million.

item.title: Market Divergence: Resilience in Energy & IT Amidst Geopolitical Storm
item.description: Last week's trading saw the BSE benchmark plummet 2.91% amid escalating West Asian geopolitical tensions and rising crude oil prices, severely impacting investor sentiment. While major banks and conglomerates like State Bank of India, ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank, and Larsen & Toubro experienced significant market capitalization erosion, Reliance Industries and Infosys emerged as notable exceptions, posting gains. This divergence highlights a growing sectoral divide, with large-cap energy and IT firms demonstrating a degree of insulation from broader market volatility.

item.title: Whale Sell-Off Sparks Retail Buying Frenzy: Bitcoin Stalemate
item.description: Bitcoin's market is in a precarious state as large holders (whales) offloaded approximately 66% of their recent accumulations following a price surge past $74,000. This profit-taking maneuver coincided with smaller retail investors steadily increasing their positions during price dips. Analytics firm Santiment flagged this divergence—whales selling while retail buys—as a classic bearish signal, suggesting the current correction is far from over. Data indicates that around 43% of Bitcoin's supply is held by underwater investors, forming a significant supply wall. The Crypto Fear and Greed Index has plunged to 18, deep within 'extreme fear' territory, reflecting pervasive investor anxiety. Despite substantial intra-week volatility, Bitcoin has shown minimal net monthly progress, oscillating between $60,000 and $74,000, currently trading around $67,451.73 as of March 8, 2026. Whale behavior points to a potential downside resolution, with large holders seemingly betting on the testing of the $60,000 support level.

item.title: Garware Technical Fibres Eyes Growth via Infra Capex, Salmon Demand
item.description: Garware Technical Fibres presented an investor overview focusing on significant growth opportunities driven by burgeoning global salmon demand and substantial infrastructure capital expenditure in India. The company highlighted its strengths in R&D, global reach, and financial stability, aiming to leverage its solutions in these expanding markets.

item.title: India's EV Push Hits Wall: PLI Scheme's High Bars Exclude Startups
item.description: Euler Motors' Founder & CEO Saurav Kumar is urging India to revise its automotive Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, arguing its stringent revenue (Rs 10,000 crore) and investment (Rs 3,000 crore fixed assets) criteria exclude promising electric vehicle startups. The company, which has invested approximately ₹1,500 crore and plans further capital infusion, contends these thresholds act as an 'innovation tax,' jeopardizing the nation's green mobility objectives by favoring established giants over agile innovators. This call highlights a critical tension between boosting large-scale manufacturing and nurturing disruptive startup ecosystems.

item.title: India's Women Investors: A $2.8 Trillion Market Imperative
item.description: India's financial ecosystem is undergoing a seismic shift as women increasingly drive household investment decisions and market participation. With an estimated 75 million working women poised to command a market worth INR 2.8 lakh crore, financial institutions face an urgent imperative to adapt from a male-centric 'access' model to a household-centric 'adoption' strategy. While women are demonstrating greater financial autonomy and a preference for long-term, goal-oriented investing, persistent challenges in financial literacy, digital access, and ingrained societal biases require a recalibration of product development and client engagement approaches.

item.title: India's Apparel Exports: Navigating Tariffs, Chasing Markets
item.description: Indian apparel exporters are experiencing a degree of relief as US tariffs on their goods have receded from peak levels. However, the volatile trade environment has amplified strategic challenges, compelling businesses to renegotiate terms, absorb margin pressures, and actively seek diversification beyond the historically dominant US market. Competitors like Vietnam and Bangladesh are leveraging their positions while global consumer demand remains sensitive to pricing.

item.title: Coal India: Dividend Yield vs. Green Pivot Capital Strain
item.description: Coal India (CIL) maintains its position as a dominant coal producer, underpinning a robust dividend yield of approximately 6.02% and strong cash flows. However, the company faces margin pressures and the considerable capital demands of its ambitious diversification into renewable energy and coal-to-chemical projects. With its Price-to-Earnings ratio near a five-year high and mixed analyst sentiment, the sustainability of its attractive dividend yield warrants scrutiny against these evolving strategic imperatives and capital allocation challenges.

item.title: DIIs Bet Big on Microcaps Amidst Market Plunge: 5 Stocks Gain Institutional Focus
item.description: Domestic institutional investors are increasing stakes in select microcap stocks, showing confidence amidst broader market declines. Five companies, including Ujjivan Small Finance Bank and Tega Industries, saw significant DII stake hikes in Q3FY26, signaling potential growth drivers despite geopolitical headwinds.

item.title: India's Luxury Car Leasing Boom: Strategic Shift in Executive Mobility
item.description: Corporate car leasing in India is evolving beyond a perk into a strategic financial tool, fueling demand for luxury vehicles. Driven by executive aspirations and companies leveraging leasing for optimized compensation and capital deployment, this trend lowers entry barriers to premium brands. The market is expanding significantly, supported by tax advantages for employees and employers, making it a sophisticated mobility solution that outpaces direct luxury car sales growth. Key players are expanding their fleets, anticipating continued strong performance in this burgeoning segment.

item.title: Fino Payments Bank Appoints Interim CEO Post-CEO's GST Arrest; RBI Approves
item.description: Fino Payments Bank has appointed Ketan Merchant as Interim CEO, effective February 27, 2026, for up to three months. This follows the arrest of its MD & CEO, Rishi Gupta, in a Goods and Services Tax (GST) evasion probe. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has approved Mr. Merchant's interim appointment, ensuring operational continuity while investigations proceed. The move aims to maintain stability amid regulatory scrutiny and ongoing assessments of Mr. Gupta's position.
